metadata.description: Salesforce AI Search - Requirements-Based Test Tracking

foundation.description: Core RAG system capabilities from original POC

requirements.title: Natural Language Query with Citations
requirements.user_story: As a Salesforce user, I want to ask natural language questions about my Salesforce data

acceptance_criteria.description: RAG System returns streaming answer with citations when user submits query
acceptance_criteria.verified_by: Browser E2E - Query: 'Available office space in Des Peres' returned structured answer with citation

acceptance_criteria.description: RAG System includes paragraph-level citations with record IDs
acceptance_criteria.verified_by: Browser E2E - Citations drawer showed: ascendix__Availability__c/a0Dfk000002JsPiEAK with Score: 1.00

acceptance_criteria.description: RAG System returns no-answer message when zero citations after filtering
acceptance_criteria.verified_by: Browser E2E - Query: 'Available Class A office space in Plano' returned 'I don't have enough information' with explanation of what WAS found

acceptance_criteria.description: Supports queries spanning Account, Opportunity, Case, Note, Property, Lease, Contract
LWC displays streaming tokens as they arrive

requirements.title: Sharing Rules and Field-Level Security
requirements.user_story: As a Salesforce administrator, I want the system to enforce Salesforce sharing rules and FLS

acceptance_criteria.description: RAG System filters results using sharing buckets for requesting user
RAG System filters results using FLS profile tags
AuthZ Sidecar caches results for 24 hours per user
RAG System performs post-filter validation on top-K results
Uses redacted chunk variants for users without field access
Zero security leaks in red-team testing

requirements.notes: Extended by zero-config-production R5-R6 for dynamic AuthZ

requirements.title: Cross-Entity Questions
requirements.user_story: As a Salesforce user, I want answers to cross-entity questions

acceptance_criteria.description: RAG System retrieves chunks from all referenced object types for multi-hop questions
Uses hybrid search combining dense embeddings and BM25
Correctly answers at least 2 multi-hop questions in acceptance testing
Stores parent relationship IDs in chunk metadata
Returns default of 8 top-K results per query

requirements.notes: Superseded by graph-aware-zero-config-retrieval R4 for graph traversal

requirements.title: Private Networking
requirements.user_story: As a security officer, I want all communication to use private networking

acceptance_criteria.description: Accepts requests only through Private API Gateway via PrivateLink
Receives traffic through Salesforce Private Connect
Encrypts data at rest using AWS KMS
Stores no data in public S3 buckets
Applies Bedrock Guardrails for prompt injection, PII, grounding

requirements.title: Data Freshness via CDC
requirements.user_story: As a sales representative, I want search results to reflect recent changes

acceptance_criteria.description: CDC Pipeline ingests change events within 5 minutes at P50
Streams data from AppFlow to S3 to EventBridge to Bedrock KB
Uses scheduled Apex export as fallback
Exposes metrics showing lag between ingest and index
Stores lastModified timestamps in chunk metadata

requirements.notes: Extended by graph-aware-zero-config-retrieval R10 with p95 < 10 min SLO

requirements.title: Monitoring and Performance Targets
requirements.user_story: As a system operator, I want to monitor retrieval quality, latency, and authorization denials

acceptance_criteria.description: Achieves p95 first token latency of 800ms or less
Achieves p95 end-to-end answer latency of 4.0s or less
Achieves retrieval precision@5 of 70% or greater
Exposes CloudWatch metrics for latency, precision, hit rate, auth denials
Triggers alarms on backlog, 5xx rate, OpenSearch degradation

requirements.notes: Extended by graph-aware-zero-config-retrieval R7 with p95 retrieval <=1.0s

requirements.title: Chunk Sizing and Metadata
requirements.user_story: As a data engineer, I want chunks properly sized and enriched

acceptance_criteria.description: Creates chunks between 300-500 tokens
Retains heading context in each chunk
Stores required metadata: sobject, recordId, parentIds, ownerId, etc.
Supports indexing up to 100,000 chunks in POC
Uses Titan Text Embeddings v2 model

requirements.title: Automated Acceptance Testing
requirements.user_story: As a QA engineer, I want automated acceptance tests for the curated query set

acceptance_criteria.description: Provides automated test framework for 22 curated CRE test queries
Measures and reports precision@5 for each query category
Returns exit code 0 if overall precision@5 >= 70%
Reports latency metrics (avg, P95, max)
Logs expected vs actual results for debugging failures

requirements.title: Planner Layer
requirements.user_story: As a CRE professional, I want my natural language queries automatically translated into structured filters

acceptance_criteria.description: Planner emits structured plan with targetObject, predicates, traversalPlan, seedIds, confidence
Planner completes analysis within 500ms (p95) with fallback
Falls back to hybrid vector search when confidence below threshold
Logs planner I/O, filters, hits/misses, latency for telemetry

requirements.title: Entity and Field Linking
requirements.user_story: As a CRE professional, I want the system to understand my terminology (Class A, office, vacancy)

acceptance_criteria.description: Entity Linker builds vocabulary from SF Describe API, picklist values, RecordTypes, layouts
Maps entity references (office, retail, Class A) to correct objects/fields
Uses field relevance scoring for ambiguous terms
Rebuilds vocabulary nightly from Describe, picklists, RecordTypes, layouts

requirements.title: Value Normalization
requirements.user_story: As a CRE professional, I want to use natural language for dates and ranges

acceptance_criteria.description: Converts temporal expressions (next 6 months, last 30 days, Q3 2026) to date ranges
Converts size ranges (20k-50k sf) to numeric filter predicates
Converts percentage values (vacancy >25%) to numeric comparisons
Maps stage/status references to valid picklist values
Expands geographic references (PNW, downtown) to matching values

requirements.title: Graph Traversal Planning
requirements.user_story: As a CRE professional, I want to query across related objects

acceptance_criteria.description: Traversal Planner determines optimal path with depth 0-2 and node/edge caps
Enforces max depth 2, max nodes 50-100, timeout limits
Returns partial results and logs cap trigger when limits exceeded
Completes traversal within 400ms (p95)

requirements.title: Derived Views and Rollups
requirements.user_story: As a CRE professional, I want to query aggregated data without slow runtime calculations

acceptance_criteria.description: Maintains availability_view with size, status, TI hints, property link
Maintains vacancy_view with vacancy % and available sqft per property
Maintains leases_view with end dates, tenant info, extracted flags
Maintains activities_agg with counts for 7/30/90 day windows
Maintains sales_view with stage, close date, broker info
Uses derived views instead of runtime aggregation
Falls back to vector+filter when rollup missing, logs gap

requirements.title: Entity Resolution
requirements.user_story: As a CRE professional, I want to search by name and find exact records

acceptance_criteria.description: Entity Resolver resolves names to SF record IDs using exact/approximate matching
Uses resolved IDs as seed filters to reduce text matches
Returns most relevant matches based on context and recency for multiple matches

requirements.title: Query Execution Performance
requirements.user_story: As a CRE professional, I want my queries to execute quickly

acceptance_criteria.description: Filters KB by recordId when Planner provides seedIds
Applies filters to both KB and graph when no seedIds
Achieves p95 retrieval latency <=1.0s (<=1.5s with graph path)
Less than 2% of results depend solely on name-text matches

requirements.title: Fallback and Reliability
requirements.user_story: As a CRE professional, I want the system to always return results even when advanced features fail

acceptance_criteria.description: Gracefully falls back to hybrid vector search when Planner fails/times out
Falls back to vector-only when graph traversal fails
Maintains fallback-to-vector-only rate below 10%

requirements.title: Quality Metrics
requirements.user_story: As a system administrator, I want to monitor search quality

acceptance_criteria.description: Achieves precision@5 >= 0.75
Maintains empty-result rate below 8%
Achieves binding precision >= 0.90 and recall >= 0.90 on test set

requirements.title: Data Freshness
requirements.user_story: As a CRE professional, I want search results to reflect recent data changes

acceptance_criteria.description: CDC pipeline propagates changes with p95 latency under 10 minutes
Nightly backfill completes within 2 hours
Refreshes rollups as part of CDC and backfill processes

requirements.title: Security and Access Control
requirements.user_story: As a system administrator, I want the system to enforce proper access controls

acceptance_criteria.description: Requires API key authentication via Named Credential only
AOSS encrypts data with customer-managed keys (CMK)
IAM policies scoped to single collection/index with env-suffixed tables
Enforces authorization at each hop during graph traversal

requirements.title: Observability and Monitoring
requirements.user_story: As a system administrator, I want comprehensive dashboards and alerts

acceptance_criteria.description: Dashboards display planner confidence, binding precision@k, fallback rate, empty-result rate, latency
Dashboards display CDC lag, rollup freshness, AOSS OCU usage, Bedrock invocations
Triggers alert when CDC lag exceeds 10 minutes
Triggers alert when fallback rate spikes or latency regresses
Logs include planner I/O, filters, hits/misses, user corrections, rollup gaps, traversal caps

requirements.title: Acceptance Scenarios
requirements.user_story: As a QA engineer, I want defined acceptance scenarios to validate the system

acceptance_criteria.description: Available Class A office space in Plano - filters Property.RecordType=Office, Class=A, City=Plano
Available industrial properties in Miami 20k-50k sf - correct filters applied
Class A office buildings downtown with vacancy >0 - correct filters
Leases expiring in next 6 months - EndDate within 6 months
Activities on Property 123 Main Street last 30 days - resolves Property by address
Contacts with >=5 activities last week and Sale stage=Negotiation
Sales where Broker=Jane Doe and Stage=Due Diligence
Companies owning >=10 retail properties in PNW
Properties with vacancy rate >25% - uses vacancy_view
Notes containing HVAC system needs replacement - text search with parent IDs

requirements.title: Schema Cache Traceability (Gap 1 Remediation)
requirements.user_story: As a system administrator, I want every field in schema cache traceable to actual Salesforce schema

acceptance_criteria.description: Every field validated against SF Describe API when populated
Source (SF Describe API response) logged when field added
Manual seeding includes validation step to verify fields exist

requirements.notes: Critical gap fix - 40% of schema cache was fabricated fields

requirements.title: Schema Discovery Integration (Gap 2 Remediation)
requirements.user_story: As a system administrator, I want schema discovery to run automatically

acceptance_criteria.description: Schema Discovery Lambda runs when new SF org connected
Calls SF Describe API for all configured objects
Runs before query processing when cache empty or stale (>24h)

requirements.notes: Critical gap fix - Schema Discovery Lambda was never invoked

requirements.title: Signal Harvesting from Saved Searches
requirements.user_story: As a CRE professional, I want the system to understand which fields are important based on Saved Searches

acceptance_criteria.description: Signal Harvester queries ascendix_search__Search__c for active saved searches
Extracts filter fields from fieldsList[].logicalName with relevance_score >= 10
Extracts result columns with relevance_score >= 5
Extracts relationship paths and marks as primary graph edges
Seeds Vocab Cache with saved search filter values
Gracefully degrades when Ascendix Search package not installed

requirements.title: Planner Relevance-Based Disambiguation
requirements.user_story: As a CRE professional, I want the system to prefer commonly-used fields when my query is ambiguous

acceptance_criteria.description: Entity Linker prefers field with highest relevance_score for multiple matches
Traversal Planner prefers relationships marked as 'primary' from saved searches
Boosts confidence when using high-relevance fields (score >= 8)
Logs disambiguation decisions with field names and scores

superseded_requirements.title: Cross-Object Query Improvements (Phase 2.5)
superseded_requirements.reason: Graph-aware retrieval provides more comprehensive relationship traversal

superseded_requirements.title: Custom Object Configuration (Phase 3)
superseded_requirements.reason: Zero-config schema discovery removes need for manual IndexConfiguration

superseded_requirements.title: Multi-Hop Relationship Queries
superseded_requirements.reason: Graph-aware zero-config provides unified graph traversal planning

superseded_requirements.title: Query Intent Router
superseded_requirements.reason: Planner layer provides comprehensive intent->plan translation

superseded_requirements.title: Auto-Discover Salesforce Object Schemas
superseded_requirements.reason: Graph-aware spec includes integrated schema discovery with gap remediation

superseded_requirements.title: Real Salesforce Sharing Rules
superseded_requirements.reason: Graph-aware spec includes authorization with per-hop enforcement
